title: Units, layers and activations

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for Units, layers and activations; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Artificial Intelligence; August 2023; Machine Learning and Neural Networks: units, optimization, generalization, and architecture families; atomic topic: Units, layers and activations
sources.claim: A feed-forward neural unit computes a weighted sum plus bias and applies an activation; layers compose these transformations into representations and outputs with explicit shapes.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A feed-forward neural unit computes a weighted sum plus bias and applies an activation; layers compose these transformations into representations and outputs with explicit shapes.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Two inputs feed two hidden ReLU units and one linear output; every weighted sum, activation, and tensor shape is calculated by hand. Your goal is to compute and visualize a forward pass through a tiny feed-forward network. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Supervised learning; Data and experimental workflow; Regression; Classification and probabilities. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: A feed-forward neural unit computes a weighted sum plus bias and applies an activation; layers compose these transformations into representations and outputs with explicit shapes. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: Weights are learned parameters not explanations, nonlinear activations enable more than one linear map, shape errors can broadcast silently, output activation depends on task, and a tiny trace does not explain large networks. The learner must reject this overclaim: Each hidden unit learns one human-readable concept and adding more linear layers always creates nonlinear decision boundaries.

includes: Trace weighted sums, nonlinear activations and composed representations through a small network.
Compute and visualize a forward pass through a tiny feed-forward network.
excludes: Weights are learned parameters not explanations, nonlinear activations enable more than one linear map, shape errors can broadcast silently, output activation depends on task, and a tiny trace does not explain large networks.

outcomes: Compute and visualize a forward pass through a tiny feed-forward network.

examples.title: Units, layers and activations: stress and transfer case
examples.situation: Removing all nonlinear activations collapses stacked dense layers into one affine transformation despite additional parameters.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name the changed population, input, assumption, or failure: Removing all nonlinear activations collapses stacked dense layers into one affine transformation despite additional parameters.

Step 2 — Keep the non-claim visible: Weights are learned parameters not explanations, nonlinear activations enable more than one linear map, shape errors can broadcast silently, output activation depends on task, and a tiny trace does not explain large networks.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting overclaim: Each hidden unit learns one human-readable concept and adding more linear layers always creates nonlinear decision boundaries.
